entry.title: Re: Watch the SAP BW Modernization Webinar Series
entry.content: Interesting stuff, however, as of unit 3 the videos fail to load (at my end at least).

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

I ran a custom code analysis using S/4 HANA 2023 FPS01 OnPrem system for a remote ECC 6.0 EHP8 development system. The SUSG usage data was collected in a production system and transferred by download/upload to the checked ECC 6.0 system.

While creatung the CCM analysis in the Fiori app I added the susg snapshot data in "Usage Configuration"

After the analysis finished the project is ready for scoping. I expected to see objects scoped by usage data, but there are no objects of this type.

Did I do something wrong? Do you have any ides what to do or what to check?
Should I contact SAP support?

Thanks and regards
Jens

entry.title: Re: SAP上海客户成功日-6.19号SAP Labs China
entry.content: 支持！

entry.title: Re: Pricing in Service Order
entry.content:
Hi,

Here is a blog that may help you on the pricing configuration for service.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/pricing-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-service/ba-p/13552129

You can refer to 3KV Create Service Product of Type "Service" and BNZ Create New Open MM Posting Period (BNZ). You can create non-stock materials using 31Y Create Product Master of type "Non-Stock Material". 

SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Create your own master data

Warm regards,

Dequan

entry.title: List of standard content in SAC for Finance/Procurement/EAM/HR on S4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new in SAC configuration and need some help regarding few questions

1-List of standard reports in finance/EAM/Procurement/HR where source is S4HANA Cloud

2-List of activities to be performed for start using SAC from Basis/Network/Security team

Thanks in advance,

Shefali

entry.title: Re: Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token...
entry.content:
Hi @satya-dev ,

Looking at your provided snippet, the following seems to be wrong:

    headers.authorization = cds.context?.headers || req?.headers

I think you meant to set it to headers.authorization.

You need to make sure, that inside your remote-service handler, the authorization header is correctly set.

Also, you should await your remote service call:

await sendToSAP(req, customID)

Best regards,
David

entry.title: Re: 欢迎！
entry.content: Kudos to Alex

entry.title: Re: what is KDS in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Hi Sandy,

sorry - I'm not familiar with the term KDS.
In case you are looking into the key entities, the majority can be found in the business configuration. Detailed structures/fields are documented in the API specification in the Business Accelerator Hub.

  1. "For an overview of the available settings, see Business Configuration in the Setup and Administration Guide. The relevant settings for each feature are also described in the current Features Overview. You can learn about the Business Configuration API in the API Guide"

Additional important entities are the rate plan templates and the pricing scheme, price element specifcations and look-up tables on the pricing side.

Good information sources in general:

  1. Learning Journey "Applying SAP Subscription Billing"
  2. Configuration Guide "Pricing with Pricing Schemes" 
  3. Video Library 

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: BTP博客索引
entry.content:
BTP

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)的架构理解 
  • BTP Acceleration加速服务介绍 

Integration Suite (CPI)

  • SAP Integration Suite集成套件问答FAQ 
  • SAP集成套件（CPI）证书更新手册 

Document Management Service (DMS)

  • 如何开始SAP BTP文档管理服务Document Management Service (DMS) 

Build Process Automation

  • 如何利用SAP Build Process Automation构建一个工作坊申请 

Build Apps

  • 如何开始SAP BTP无代码服务 - 构建一个端到端的BP展示页面 

Build Work Zone

  • UI Integration Card to S/4 on SAP Build Work Zone Standard Version

Identity Authentication Service

  • SAP Cloud Identity now available for all RISE with SAP customers

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

  • SAP Analytics cloud SAML SSO with BTP Cloud Identity services - Identity Authentication End to End From SAP Analytics cloud to the Live BW system

entry.title: FTXP Maintain Tax Rates - Do we have Data migration cockpit object available in S4 HANA?
entry.content:
Hi, We are on S4 HANA 2023 version and looking for ways to load tax rates for tax conditions (FTXP) 

I have looked at Condition record for pricing (general template). This is not working as we need to maintain condition Key fields. 

Is there any other DMC object that is delivered for this? Any other ways to load?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to combine multiple Fiori floor plans...
entry.content:
@livingston04 

If your OData service version is V4 (with draft enabled in the edit scenario), you can use the Flexible Programming Model to combine freestyle and Fiori elements.

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/overview/introduction

You can either start with the List Report template and add custom pages, or start with the Basic template and add a List Report-like page using building blocks.

Hi,

Here the information may help you.

Warm regards,

Dequan

entry.title: Re: IAG Bridge Scenario - GRC AC 12.0 integration with IAG
entry.content:
Hi Muthu,

Thank you for the wonderful blog!

I have a question-

We will be using GRC AC and IAG Bridge to extend the current GRC functionality for Access Management.
There will be Hybrid approach where Access request will be provisioned through GRC if its for On-premise system and through IAG if its for Cloud Apps. Initial request will be triggered from GRC only.

Now I would like to know what will be feasible-
1. Creating Business roles in IAG for cloud apps and then sync it with GRC, in GRC job roles for On-premise system are already available. So create another Business role in GRC which will contain Job role of On-premise and Business role from IAG.

or

2. Create a job role in IAG for cloud apps and include all the job-roles from On-prem in the same business role via sync. Is this possible in IAG bridge scenario?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Production order confirmation via custom design
entry.content:
Hello @alex_alex85 

I think you are facing 3 different kinds of potential issues in your process:

  1. Permanent or semi-permanent errors e.g. a user tries to post a confirmation for a non existing production order or for an order that is not released yet
  2. Transient locking errors i.e. a user tries to post a confirmation for an order that is being processed and thus is locked
  3. Material movements posting errors, i.e. COGI entries

In my opinion there is no silver bullet solution or "one ring to rule them all". So all look at them one by one.

Permanent or semi-permanent errors You can implement validations in your custom application to prevent this kind of errors. Also make sure to report messages coming back from BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT so a user know if confirmation has been posted or not. Also consider using TESTRUN parameter to quickly verify data provided to the BAPI.

A verification to consider is to check if a user tries to post the same confirmation i.e. for the same production order, for the same quantity, etc. multiple times within short time. That might indicate that they are posting duplicates.

Transient locking errors Here you can use POST_WRONG_ENTRIES parameter to decide if confirmations failed due to locking conflicts should be rejected or saved in the error pool for reprocessing with CO16N:

The approach would depend on the nature of your confirmation process. If you have a high volume process and want to allow parallel confirmation postings for the same production order, go with POST_WRONG_ENTRIES = 2 to save erroneous confirmations in the error pool. Notice however that this will save both confirmation with locking conflicts and with exceptions to the pool.

Material movements posting errors Due to its design BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT can post material movements synchronously in the same LUW together with confirmations. Therefore COGI entries are unavoidable in case of material movements posting errors. I think the best approach here is to educate users about that and teach them to verify confirmations in production orders, not material movements as they can still be waiting for reprocessing in COGI.

In any case, I think running CORUPROC every 5 minutes is too frequent. You are introducing more locking conflicts this way.

Last point, educate the users that if your application posts a confirmation successfully, they don't need to verify that and might not see the confirmation or material movements in the production order immediately. The confirmation might have been written to the error pool for latter processing. Of course a production admin has to monitor the error pool with CO16N and COGI.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Dump while getting data from custom table using RAP a...
entry.content:
@michael_scofield 

Have you activated the class ZBP_ZCDS_2?

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Jens,

it looks like this scoping is done based on usage data: the Not in scope objects are not used objects, the In scope (by object) – objects, for which usage data do not exist, for example DDIC objects like Data Elements. Such objects are always automatically in the scope.In scope (by reference) – objects, which are statically referenced by the objects in the scope.

In the general project overview you should see "Scoped Objects":

Do you see it?

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change setting of list report filter?
entry.content:
@webhua 

The following configuration may be relevant.

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#paging-limits

entry.title: Re: Joys and sorrows of the ABAP Developer Tools API
entry.content:
If you are still interested in this topic, please consider supporting the following Customer Influence improvement request:

Make ADT API documentation available to the ABAP developer community

entry.title: Re: Cost Centre Segreggation
entry.content:
Cost centers are cost drivers that carry the costs for any department, location, or person. There is a structure developed at the Controlling Area level called the Standard Hierarchy. For instance, if the Electricity department requires separate reporting, you can create a new node in the existing Controlling Area hierarchy. This node, also known as a cost center group, can be used for reporting and allocation purposes.

If you need to change any details of a cost center, you can modify specific attributes. However, you cannot change integrated objects like the profit center or cost element category once costs have been posted.

For further understanding, you can explore free learning content on SAP.

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-overhead-cost-controlling-in-sap-s-4hana/using-internal-orders_fb96cf93-18ac-4c8e-96fc-c63b65f2af0f

BR

Abid Aqeel

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change setting of list report filter?
entry.content: Thank you Mio. I have done a test according to this guideline, it only affects the table data, not drop-down filter.

entry.title: Experience Innovation and Sustainability: Introducing the SAP Energy Park
entry.content:
In the face of growing environmental challenges and the urgent need for sustainable energy practices, SAP is setting a powerful example in driving the energy transition. Through a combination of innovative technology solutions, strategic partnerships, and a commitment to sustainability, SAP aims to not only transform its own operations but also empower other organisations to achieve their energy transition goals.

Standing at the forefront with its pioneering SAP Energy Park in Walldorf, Germany, this initiative exemplifies SAP’s commitment to sustainability and showcases how cutting-edge technology can be harnessed to create a greener future. In this blog you will have a look at what makes the SAP Energy Park a model for sustainable energy solutions.

 

The Urgent Need for Transition

In recent years, the escalating concerns about the consequences of climate change and the environmental repercussions of traditional energy sources have gained significant attention globally. The root cause of these changes is closely linked to our reliance on traditional energy sources, such as coal, oil, and natural gas. One of the most significant environmental impacts of traditional energy sources is the emission of greenhouse gases (GHGs), particularly carbon dioxide (CO2).

By embracing renewable energy, enhancing energy efficiency, and supporting innovative policies, we can mitigate the adverse effects of climate change and protect our planet for future generations. The time to act is now, and every step taken towards sustainability counts

In an era where sustainability is not just a buzzword but a business imperative, SAP is leading the charge with a robust strategy focused on creating a sustainable future. As a global leader in enterprise application software, SAP recognizes the critical role it plays in driving sustainability across industries.

 

The Rise of Prosumers in the Energy Sector

The energy industry is undergoing a profound transformation, characterized by a significant shift where industries are not just consumers but also producers of energy. This paradigm shift is driven by technological advancements, regulatory changes, and the pressing need for sustainable practices. As a result, the traditional boundaries between energy producers and consumers are becoming increasingly blurred.

The traditional model of centralized energy production, where large power plants generate electricity and distribute it to consumers, is being challenged by a more decentralized approach. Advances in renewable energy technologies, such as solar panels, wind turbines, and energy storage systems, enable businesses to generate their own electricity on-site. This shift is fostering the rise of "prosumers" – entities that both produce and consume energy.

The cost of renewable energy technologies has dropped significantly over the past decade, making them more accessible to businesses. Solar and wind power installations are now viable options for companies looking to reduce their carbon footprint and energy costs.

Digital technologies and smart grids play a crucial role in managing decentralized energy systems. Smart meters, sensors, and advanced analytics enable real-time monitoring and optimization of energy usage and production. These technologies facilitate demand response, where industries can adjust their energy consumption based on grid conditions, thereby enhancing overall grid stability and efficiency.

 

Leading the Change towards Energy Transition with Innovative Solutions

SAP’s dedication to sustainability is reflected in its ambitious goals and comprehensive strategy. The company aims to achieve net-zero emissions across its entire value chain by 2030. This commitment is not just about reducing its own carbon footprint but also about helping other businesses track, manage, and reduce their emissions.

SAP recognizes the importance of decentralized energy production in the transition to sustainable energy systems. By leveraging advanced technologies and digital solutions, is helping industries become active participants in energy production. SAP’s solutions help optimize these systems, ensuring that energy is used efficiently and improving the condition of their assets.

 

The SAP Energy Park Initiative

The Walldorf Campus is a testament to SAP’s commitment to sustainability, where visitors can explore firsthand how SAP’s latest technological advancements and innovative solutions integrates into every aspect of its operations.

These showcases highlight real-world applications, installations and demonstrate the potential for your organization to leverage these innovations for enhanced efficiency and growth:

At the SAP Experience Center customers have the chance to explore the transformative journey from energy consumers to advance prosumers. With the S.MART Energy Shop showcase, customers and partners embark in firsthand on a transformation journey that navigates the intersection of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs), the rise of the prosumer, and the power of digital innovation.

E-Mobility is a crucial puzzle piece for companies and individuals to reduce emissions and to achieve net zero. Many industries such as Oil, Gas & Energy, Utilities or Automotive see the value in redefining their business models resulting in new growth opportunities as charging offerings need to be where people spent time. Energy Retail is the case to demonstrate SAP’s industry convergent capabilities combining and uplifting a shopping experience with EV charging to gain additional loyalty benefits.

Power Plants are core assets for Utilities and Energy companies and are also of relevance for industrial customers to provide local and resilient energy. Monitoring the asset health, predictive maintenance and an active failure management are fundamental to handle the assets at low cost and to avoid outages. The SAP WDF50 is the power plant located in Walldorf which maintains and controls cooling and heating units at office. This is a real industrial case which enables and digitalize the Power Plant with the help of SAP Solutions.

Solar power generation is increasingly being adopted by every industry, with the goal of having renewable energy self-supply. The SAP Energy Park is equipped with an extensive array of solar panels, harnessing the power of the sun to generate clean, renewable energy. We plan to leverage these installations to demonstrate scenarios in a real-world environment that help to evaluate the energy produced, to manage investment and potentially play a role in flexibility markets.

E-Vehicles are a key factor for Energy Transition, it will not be possible without intelligence applied to the Charging Points and the EV Batteries. We want to demonstrate the capability of SAP to manage and optimize the recharge of its own fleet of EVs (mostly company cars for employees) and the overall energy consumption.

Energy is the feed for every business enterprise, and consuming it wisely is what makes a difference to achieve sustainability goals. This requires an 360° data overview on how the Energy Park is performing from energy generation and consumption perspective as well as the perspective on ESG reporting.

Having private homes as new suppliers in the energy market is a new business model for energy retailers. Our goal is to establish SAP Energy Park as a pioneering model of an Energy Community in Germany, where prosumer-employees can seamlessly sell excess renewable energy directly to SAP, showcasing the transformative potential of SAP solutions and encouraging customers to adopt similar practices.

 

Conclusion

The SAP Energy Park in Walldorf is a shining example of how technology and innovation can drive sustainable energy practices. Is not only supporting SAP’s own sustainability goals but also serves as an inspiration and model for other organizations worldwide how to thrive towards their goals of zero emissions.

It offers unparalleled networking opportunities with industry peers/experts, SAP partners, and other customers. Share experiences, exchange ideas, and build valuable connections that can lead to future collaborations and business opportunities.

Take this opportunity to see firsthand how SAP is shaping the future of business.

entry.title: Problem -Unable to call protected app from another App.
entry.content:
Hi Expert,
I have created two CAP applications, each with its own XSUAA authentication service. I want to call one CAP service from another CAP application. I followed @CarlosRoggan  blog here, which worked perfectly for external users but failed for the application-to-application case.

I followed the exact steps, but the user JWT token doesn't contain the provider app scope when doing it for another application. However, it works perfectly for external users. Please help me.
@CarlosRoggan @gregorw @MioYasutake @Ivan-Mirisola 

Thanks
Somnath

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Customer Engagement for Utilities - Process Overview
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

It took a while to think about posting this content; this blog is about SAP S/4 HANA IS-U CS/Customer Engagement processes; I humbly request you to please help and support as I am not that expert in blogging. Also, your feedback & comments are very welcome so I will come with more accurate data next time, let’s start:

S/4 HANA IS-U CS/CE Overview:

  1. What are Utilities?
  • Utilities are providers of essential services like:
    • Electricity
    • Gas
    • Water and waste
    • Heat
  • Utility consumers include:
    • Individuals
    • Industries
    • Organizations

Organizations that provide these essential services are called utilities and the industry is called utility industry.

  1. Utilities Industry Segmentation
    • Industry Segmentation

 

  • Common Factors:
    • All produce, distribute & sell.
    • The customer service structure is similar.
    • Service setup & maintenance is similar in concept.
    • Planning is similar.
    • Weather is a demand factor.
  1. Utilities Sector Value Chain:
    • Electricity

 

  • Gas:

 

  • Water:

 

  1. Regulation and De-regulation in Utilities Industry:
    • Regulation:
  • Set or rules & policies set by government that utilities companies must follow to operate in the market. Regulation performs various function:
    • Sets rates.
    • Defines scope of services.
    • Prescribing accounting practices.
    • Enforces service standards & reliability of service.
    • Approves new investments.
  • De-regulation:
  • The process of removing regulations or other barriers in order to encourage efficient operation of markets. Aim of deregulation:
    • Dismantle monopolies.
    • Create competition and promote efficiencies.
    • Lower rates & cut costs.
    • Maintain reliable & efficient services.
    • Enhance choices for the customer.
  1. SAP Focus Area for Utilities:

 

  1. SAP IS-U Master Data:
    • Master Data in IS – Utilities

 

  • Business Master Data:

Any master data that is used for billing a customer for the services provided is grouped under Business data. For e.g., a business partner who is a person or an organization.

 

  • Technical Master Data:

Any master data that is used for providing the services are grouped under Technical Data. For e.g., a device or a meter that is installed in a customer location.

 

  1. Business Master Data:
    • Business Partner:
      • A natural person or legal entity, with which the Utility company has a legal relationship.
      • Contains central data such as business partner’s name, addresses, communication numbers and bank details.
      • The Business Partner in IS-U will be replicated from CRM.
  • Contract Account:
    • An account held by a business partner where the posting data for contracts takes place. Here you define Payment Methods, Dunning Procedure & Tolerances
    • The contract account defines how the customer’s account is treated, e.g., a customer’s home and business account would have different billing term.
    • Means the consumption in home would be much lower than that of plant/factory (business account) hence account would be different and different billing term would be applicable.
  • Contract:
    • Contracts are utility service agreements between the utility company and its business partners.
    • A contract is related to a division. It links one single contract account to one installation. However, several contracts can be grouped together in one contract account for which the same payment and dunning terms apply.
  1. Technical Master Data:
    • Connection Object:
      • Connection Objects in SAP are usually buildings but can also be part of a building or a facility such as streetlight or construction site.
      • Since an address is allocated to a connection object, the connection object links premises, device locations and connections to the postal regional structure.
  • Premise:
    • The premise is an enclosed spatial unit that is supplied with energy, for example, an apartment or factory.
    • A premise can have several utility installations (Supply Points) and is division independent.
    • The premise is allocated to a connection object and to the address of that connection object.
  • Installation:
    • An installation groups together the devices, registers, and, if applicable, reference values that belong to the same division and are allocated to the same premise, and that are therefore billed together, such as an on-peak and an off-peak rate meter.
    • An installation is allocated to one contract only. In exceptional cases (such as an installation under construction or an installation without an owner) an installation is not allocated to a contract

Business and Technical Master data link:

 

  • Device Location:
    • Location within a connection object where devices are installed. Device locations correspond to functional locations.
  • Device:
    • Individual, physical object that is to be maintained as an autonomous unit. Devices can be:
      • Counting devices (meters)
      • Data processing devices (converters)
      • Devices with protective or adjusting functions (pressure controllers)
  • Point of Delivery:
    • This is a globally identified unique service point where a utility service is provided. In a deregulated market (a topic in itself), there are multiple service providers providing a customer different service. For e.g., a utility company will be providing the actual electricity and any associated infrastructure around it whereas another provider will be able to provide billing, customer service and other related services
    • So, in a deregulated market, it becomes important to uniquely identify service point and it is done using the point of delivery data object. There is a 1:1 relationship between point of delivery and installation.
  • Device Category:
    • This groups the various devices into different categories based on the technical characteristics.
    • This enables us to maintain the data that is common for a group of devices, there by grouping them in a logical way. Some examples of device categories are Meter, Transformer, Pressure regulator etc.

This summarizes the key master data objects that make up the IS-U data model. There are other data objects like Technical Installation, Connection, Grid and Services that are used for augmenting these key data objects.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Re: Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi, 

I've solved the issue previously described with texts, the problem was that I created all the hierarchy structure only in tables, then when I created views over those tables and use them in the data model, the hierachy started to work fine.

 

Regards.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Customer Engagement for Utilities - Process Overview (Part-2)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm writing this blog in continuation with my previous blog on S/4 HANA IS-U Customer Engagement overview, this blog will be revolving around SAP CRM IS-U features and most likely will come back on S/4 HANA CE features with respect to SAP IS-U in my next blog, let’s start further:

Data Model:

However, below depicted picture has been referenced from traditional SAP CRM IS-U concept but the data model for S/4 HANA IS-U Customer Engagement is still the same, so we can relate it easily in S/4 HANA IS-U CE as well:

 

SAP IS-U CRM Integration Data Model:

Move-In & Move-Out Processing:

  • This function enables you to process the following:
    • Move-In
    • Move-Out
    • Move-In/Out for a premise
    • Move-In/Out for a business partner
    • Contract Change
  • Move-In:
    • You use the move-in function to carry out most of the activities required to begin providing a business partner with a utility service. You create new or change existing business master data. Most importantly, you allocate a contract to an installation.
    • In move-in processing, you establish whether a contract can be billed. From the move-in, you can also trigger the charges levied at the commencement of a utility service, as well as a welcome letter to the customer.

 Prerequisites:

  • To create a move-in, you require one or more installations that are vacant on the desired move-in date.
  • In the case of an initial move-in, ensure that you have completed initial data creation, including creation of the installation(s)
  • Devices can (but do not have to) be installed and allocated before move-in
  • If one of the installations is already allocated to a contract, this allocation must be reversed. To do this, process a move-out. An installation can only be allocated to one contract at any given time.
  • Move-Out:
    • You use the move-out function to carry out most of the activities required to terminate a business partner’s utility service. You make changes to the business master data of the business partner as necessitated by the move-out. The most important change is that the allocation of a contract to an installation is terminated on the move-out date.
    • You stop the active payment plans using move-out processing. You can also trigger a letter of move-out confirmation and an immediate bill.

Prerequisites:

  • Before you create a move-out, ensure that one or more contracts with installations exist on the desired move-out date.
  • Create move-in/out for (a) premise(s):
  • You use this function to change the business partner of the premise and/or the installations belonging to the premise.
  • To select an installation, specify a premise or a business partner. You can then create a move-out to occur on the specified move-out date and then create the subsequent move-in. This applies to all the installations you selected.
  • Create move-in/out for business partner.
    • You use this function to perform a move-in/out for a business partner within the supply area. You can create the move-out so that the business partner moves out of several installations, and the move-in so that the business partner moves into several installations. You can select the business partner using the move-out search parameters.
    • You can use the move-in search parameters to determine the installations into which the business partner is to move.
    • If the installations of the new premise are not vacant, a dialog window appears where you must specify whether you also want to create a move-out for the business partner allocated to these installations.
  • Processing an Existing Move-In/Move-Out
    • Processing a move-in or move-out once it has been created is facilitated by integrated functions that enable you to reverse billing, invoicing, and FI-CA documents. If the (new) move-out date or move-in date is in a period that has already been billed, you have the following options:
      • Create a move-out.
      • Change the move-out.
      • Reverse a move-out.
      • Reverse a move-in.
    • The system reacts by reversing the billing document. If the document has already been invoiced, then the system reverses invoicing before it reverses the billing document. Any other FI-CA documents that were generated by invoicing are also reversed. If the documents have already been cleared through payment, then the system reverses the payment clearing.
    • Any documents assigned to one of the move-in contracts are reversed first. The clearing is reversed upon payment. This is also the case if the contract has not yet been billed.

 Disconnection/Reconnection:

  • This component enables you to manage disconnections and reconnections. This component is optional. You need to use it if you want to temporarily interrupt supply to customers (especially within the bounds of a legal dunning procedure). A disconnection is a temporary interruption in the supply of a utility service that does not involve the meter removal. This is due to the following reasons, for example:
    • Introduction of a collection procedure for outstanding payment
    • Technical disconnection due to request of utilities company or customer
  • In the first instance, the service is resumed (reconnection) when the customer has paid the outstanding bill; in the second, it is done at the request of the customer.
  • The disconnection document is automatically generated when the disconnection dunning level is reached. It contains all the items that reach the disconnection dunning level during a dunning print run.
  • Generally, the devices for disconnection are proposed from all the contracts belonging to a contract account. This makes the contract account the reference object of the disconnection document.
  • The disconnection document guarantees consistency in the overall disconnection and reconnection business process. A reconnection cannot be performed without a disconnection having occurred beforehand. If a disconnection has already been carried out on a device or flat-rate installation, a new disconnection order cannot be generated for this disconnection object.
  • The disconnection/reconnection orders are confirmed in the disconnection document. They are assigned a status. Disconnection actions that have already been carried out can be reversed via the disconnection document (note that can only been done successively from the last action to the first).                                              

Owner Allocation:

  • Owner Allocation process provides an automated means of placing the service into the landlord/owner’s name between tenants.  The “Owner” is the Business Partner linked to the premise. In the current system, the Property object is the object used to link an owner/landlord to a premise. This wiki basically supports Call Centre Users to create multiple allocations.
  • Owner Allocation process provides an automated means of placing the service into the landlord/owner’s name between tenants.  The “Owner” is the Business Partner linked to the premise. In the current system, the Property object is the object used to link an owner/landlord to a premise. This development basically supports Call Centre Users to create multiple allocations.
  • A Property is an SAP object created to provide time dependent maintenance of the owner and installation. A Property will be created for each premise that the owner requests an Agreement be established for.  Each Property will have a contract account specified and this contract account will be used each time the owner is moved into the specified premise.  This way the owner’s contract accounts and contracts, will be more easily managed.  A key date field is also provided for selection according to time related validity.
  • The business requirement is to have the ability to create, transfer ownership from one BP to another, reverse or update multiple properties on Owner Allocation without having to enroll each property separately.  Large apartment complexes typically enroll on this program.

 

Front Office:

  • This component aids you in giving information to customers and in executing processes and workflows. A standard configuration is delivered with the SAP Utilities (IS-U) component. You can freely configure the front office in Customizing, meaning you can integrate all the most frequent processes and most important information in your utility company. In addition, you can define the appearance of the front office screens. In this way, an appropriate work environment can be created for each agent, and he/she can access the exact data and functions needed for his or her individual tasks.
  • The first step in the process is the identification of the customer. This is supported by the front office search function (data finder)
  • You can display the customer's data in one of the information views and, in other views, branch to more detailed information on specific areas. In this way, you can, for example, access information about bills, rates, budget billing amounts or meter reading results
  • Customizing contains a number of front office processes, using which you can process common business transactions. These include, for e.g.:
    • Changes to customer data
    • Move-in/out.
    • Interaction Record
  • If all the data required for a front office process is available, then you can deal with the customer's request immediately. Alternatively, you can temporarily interrupt the process and complete it later. The system displays uncompleted processes in the front office.
  • More complex processes, that consist of more than one processing step or do not run at once, can be controlled using an SAP Business Workflow. Based on the workflow you use; the execution of the process can be staggered- for example delayed until a specific event. It can also be handled by different agent. You can start the following workflows in the front office:
    • Create service connection.
    • Collection procedures
    • Disconnection/reconnection of an installation
  • The main differences between front office processes and workflows are as follows:
    • A front office process is as temporary entity, meaning that it is terminated as soon as the front office session is ended, in which it was created. This keeps system administration costs to a minimum and improves system response times. Moreover, you can you enhanced navigation functions, such as scrolling.
    • All the steps of a front office process are executed by one agent only, namely the agent who called the current front office session. As a result, there is no need for agent determination.
    • If necessary, a front office process can start a workflow, ensuring that the processing of the business transaction is completed. In addition, an agent can resubmit a business transaction for another agent if he/she is not equipped to deal with the customer's request. You can use the notes function to append any additional information to the resubmission.
  • Integration:
    • The front office is integrated with all other SAP Utilities components. Certain processes and workflows are integrated with SAP standard components. Some examples are illustrated in the table below. You can use processes and workflows (that use other components) yourself.

 

Process/Workflow

Standard Component

Create service connection

Service Management (PM-SMA)

Sales and Distribution (SD)

Disconnection/reconnection of an installation

Service Management (PM-SMA)

Sales and Distribution (SD)

Collection procedures

Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA)

 SAP CRM Web UI Overview:

  • It is a business role-based application:
    • This means that through this interface the user will be able to access only those applications for which he/she is having authorizations.
    • If a user is assigned a single role, he will not have to select the role on logon.
    • But in case there are multiple roles assigned to a user, he/she will have to select one of the available roles in order to log in to the CRM Web UI

 

  • Irrespective of the role selected the screens of the CRM Web UI are structured in L-Shape
  • But the applications and functionalities which user will be able to access in the CRM Web UI depends up on the role selected.
  • Once logged in, user can see the Web UI screens in L-Shape
  • It consists of following components:
    • Header Area
    • Navigation Area
    • Work Area

  • Positions of all components of the L-Shape are fixed and cannot be changed.
  • An administrator can change the skin and logo of the web client as per company specific requirements.
  • Header area is provided to access application independent URLs and functionalities.
  • Using navigation bar user can access different applications, to which he is having authorizations.
  • Work Area gets updated based on actions a user takes on the Navigation bar.
  • It also gets updated for few actions on the header area.

Header Area

    • Header area is static, and its position is fixed.
    • It is possible to set its height in the CRM customizing but its position is fixed.
    • Header area consists of following components.
      • System Links
      • Saved Searches
      • Work Area Title
      • History

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team

Today we are using Invoice List (VF21) to Consolidate the Invoice and give to the Customer on Monthly basis. Moving ahead, SAP has provided new Tcode for Invoice Consolidation - EDOC_MY_SUMMARY.

Questions :

1. Is there any Link between Invoice List and SAP Consolidated invoice program?

2. Once the Invoices are Consolidated using this program, will this be updated in the EDOC_COCKPIT as a single Line item which can be submitted to the Portal ?

3. Will there be a QR Code returned from the Porta for the Consolidated invoice ?

4. How can we generate the Output of this Consolidated Invoice ?

5. Can we have this program Customer wise ?

6. Can Consolidated invoice be used for Foreign Customers also ?

Regards Violet

entry.title: Re: I have a 3 party tool that need read access to tables...
entry.content:
Hi

Data export from BW Infoproviders for 3rd parties is only supported via OData:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/11853413cf124dde91925284133c007d.html?locale=en-US

or by OpenHub:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/4c1a299d54914c86e10000000a42189e.html?locale=en-US

 

The usage of native ODP RFC functions by 3rd parties is prohibited:

3255746 Unpermitted usage of ODP Data Replication APIs

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: Activity rate calculation
entry.content:
Hi Kishore,

To meet the requirement, record all production costs in service cost-center ABC and capture the activity quantities in production cost-center XYZ. Use an allocation process to consider costs from ABC and quantities from XYZ for the activity rate calculation. This ensures no costs are directly posted to XYZ while accurately calculating the activity rate.

Then calculate actual rate and run order revaluation to charge on production.

BR

Abid Aqeel

entry.title: Re: SAPui5 : change design of a label in a formelement
entry.content:
Customizing SAPUI5 FormElement Labels: Best Practices and Workarounds

When working with SAPUI5 applications, you may encounter a situation where you need to customize the appearance of labels in your form elements. Specifically, you might want to change the label design to bold. While there are straightforward ways to achieve this using CSS, not all methods align with SAP's best practices for styling. In this blog post, we'll explore both a recommended approach and a common workaround.

The Challenge

The challenge arises when trying to directly style the labels of FormElement controls in an SAPUI5 form. For example, consider the following code snippet where we want to make the label bold.

The direct application of CSS styles to the label is not straightforward due to the way SAPUI5 generates and manages DOM elements.

Recommended Approach: Using CSS Classes

Workaround: Using Specific Element IDs

In this case, you might need a quick and dirty solution, especially for rapid prototyping or when dealing with legacy code. Here’s a workaround that involves targeting specific element IDs:

  1. Identify the element ID using the browser debugger. You can find the ID by running the following code in the console:

                                    this.getView().byId("label1").sId

  2. Add the CSS rules to your stylesheet:in style.css file:

    #application-XXX-display-component---ViewX--label1-label-bdi {
    font-weight: bold !important;
    color: rgb(0, 0, 0) !important;
    }

    Replace application-XXX-display-component---ViewX--label1-label-bdi with the actual ID obtained from the previous step.

    xxx-your app name
    Viewx-your view
    label1-your label id

    Conclusion

    While using specific element IDs in CSS can quickly solve styling issues, it is not a recommended practice for production code due to its lack of scalability and maintainability. Instead, leveraging CSS classes provides a more robust and sustainable approach to styling SAPUI5 applications.

    By following best practices, you ensure that your SAPUI5 applications remain maintainable, scalable, and aligned with SAP’s guidelines. Remember, clean code is not just about making things work; it’s about making things work well for years to come.

    Happy coding!

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud - European Accessibility Act Support
entry.content:
Hello,

I read an article stating that the European Accessibility Act (EAA) will become a legal requirement in the EU by 2025. Consequently, I am curious to know if the composable storefront's planning includes this in their roadmap. Although I noticed that WCAG 2.0 is part of the roadmap, it is not clear if there is a specific plan for EAA compliance or if WCAG 2.0 encompasses EAA requirements as well.

Thank you.

Best regards,

Sarbajeet

entry.title: Re: When will C4C v1 be sunset?
entry.content: Thanks Alan. How can partners then better decide whether to offer v1 or v2 to a client?

entry.title: Error while creating a decisions in Build Process Automation - Again?
entry.content:
I'm trying to add a decision object to a SAP Build Process Automation project. After creating and configuring the decision I close the decision configuration screen and can see that the decision object found in the overview of objects in the project is stored with a almost transparent font.

 The decision object disappears (automatically deleted) in the overview of objects on the project.

I have raised similar question previously (Solved: Error while creating a decision - SAP Community) and the issue was solved. But it seems like the issue with decision creations in Build Process Automation occurs again. 

Has anyone done the same observations?

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne May '24 reflections
entry.content:
Last month we had our second Melbourne Stammtisch for 2024. It was a little while ago, but it's better late than never as they say 🙂

We held the event at the same location which provided the same great atmosphere and food and drinks as last time and now we are starting to get some sense of regularity, after all the idea is a SAP regulars' table.

The event was held after the Mastering SAP conference so it was a good chance to discuss topics raised for those who attended. Also it was a welcome privilege to have been joined by @thomas_jung while he was in town and get some thoughts from his perspective as well.

This was another great chance to dig into the big ideas of the SAP ecosystem and just to chat and get to know fellow members of the community.

Please leave your comments on what you want, what you enjoyed about the Melbourne Stammtisch and lets aim to meet up in July to keep the momentum.

 

 

Ben.

entry.title: Re: 0MB memory and 0 routes on Cloud Foundry trial account
entry.content: If anyone is still facing this issues the solution is to add service Cloud Foundry Runtime in your entitlements

Olga.

entry.title: Re: Production order confirmation via custom design
entry.content:
Hi Dominik, Thanks for the clarification.

Transient locking errors - this feature is already in use.

Material movements posting errors - we can consider this also.

Permanent or semi-permanent errors - there is a question. We can not reprodue CO1P entry for test purposes and can not check what we have in BAPI with CO1P. Is it known if the BAPI return something, which says that it is related to CO1P? Not to lock error, not to COGI etc. But particularly to CO1P. Or we are able to check whether the posting was done or not only after Confirmation was completed?

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Real User Monitoring business user utilization report
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly let us know is there any application to view the business user utilization (what are all the application used how frequently used and when the business user used the particular application) of the S4 PERSON_NAME public cloud system.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Real User Monitoring business user util...
entry.content:
The Real User Monitoring app has its own built-in help interface which describes the usage of the various views/tabs available in the app.
The built-in help interface can be opened by clicking the relevant icon ("?" in a circle) at the top right of the app page. 

Some apps that align with your criteria are:

  • The "Requests Overview" view/tab in the Real User Monitoring app allows you to see all requests/actions executed in your monitored systems based on the request type, including by the technical application/request name. You can drill down into each request to see more details about the actions the user took for the request/app, with timestamps and other information.
    If you need to filter data based on specific services/systems, you must make an appropriate selection in the "Scope" area of the Real User Monitoring app.
  • The "Analysis" view/tab in the Real User Monitoring app allows you to see End-2-End response times, with dimensions including the application/request name. 
  • The "Execution Flow" view/tab allows you to see detailed info about the actions of a user and the corresponding system response, in chronological order. 

Experiment with the various views/tabs on offer to identify the one that best meets your needs.

If none of the existing views/tabs meet your needs, custom dashboards or tools can be created from scratch using SAP Cloud ALM data, by consuming it using the various SAP Cloud ALM REST APIs.

entry.title: Re: Virtual element not refreshed on Save RAP SAP
entry.content:
Hi @santosh_fhalke , 

you need side effect annotations for this, cf https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/18b17bdd49d1436fa9172cbb01e26544 .

Regards,

  Wolfgang

entry.title: Change WSDL Endpoint in XML via SOAMANAGER did not work permanently
entry.content:
From external the soap interface should be available. We have on the public address an load balancer, webdispatcher and at the end the ERP System NW 750. All running in SAP RISE.

But in the buttom of the xml file I cannot change the url from internal to public in the soap:address location tag.

In the table HTTPURLLOC I can change the domain but not the port to https.

If I use the Alternative URL or Reverse Proxy the config could not saved and in another session the settings are gone. I try this also with publish service. SCC4 is open for changes.

There are some new notes 1849044 and 2993185 from the last two months – but did not helped.

Any ideas ?

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Batch Management, Decentralised EWM
entry.content: Thanks Shailesh, Will try this option and hope will meet my requirement

entry.title: Issue with address inconsistencies when synchronizing Employee to Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, I have been requested to solve an issue related to a BP in S4 on premise.

To give context: We had a BP not synchronized correctly with its employee number (from PA30).

Our functional team executed RSUID_REMOVE_FALSE_BP_ASGN to unlink them and when executing /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE to generate a new BP and link it with the employee number we received the following 2 error logs:

Message R1000 "Error_Time Dependency_Addresses ADVW_VALIDITY_ERROR"

Message R11245 "Error for validity periods of address usages"

I have checked the address' validity periods in the HR master (PA30) and the BP and they match, also I have checked BUT020, ADRC and PA0006 tables and they match too. 

Reading sap note 2578294, it suggests to review the path "Cross-Application Components=>SAP Business Partner=>Business Partner=>Basic Settings=>Address Determination" and make sure the address type XXDEFAULT is set on "Assign Transaction to Address Type", and it is. Also I have checked sap notes 2517507 and 2598468, but they cannot be implemented in the system. 

Going on, we have executed again /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE but with the flag "Override Banks, addresses and roles" and it gives us a different error log:

Message no. AM266 Personal address not deleted; other reference exists.

Searching for it I have found this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-questions/bupa-del-error/qaq-p/10741420, it says that the error generally occurs for the addresses  of table BCST_CAM, I have checked it and there isn't any entry for the employee number, could that be the problem? Going trough note 835936, I have executed RSBCS_ADDRESS_REFERENCE, but it says that there isn't any reference. 

Does someone know what else can I do? Or how should I proceed? 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) for Developers in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

I execute the ATC in Eclipse from the 7.58 SAP System by using a check variant in 7.58 that contains the check Syntax Check in Remote System that points to the 7.40 SAP System.


I've also extracted the log from the Eclipse ABAP Communication Log:

Thank you,
Alfonso.

entry.title: Re: Errors in standard CDS view C_PRODUCT
entry.content:
We solved the error by reverting object to previous state in Version management.

It happened to us because original version (the newest version) was overwritten by the old version during upgrade transport.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Hi David and everyone,

I just wanted to say that my learning journey with SAP has been challenging. I initially joined a bootcamp last year to study SAP implementation (public cloud) with activate and I quickly discovered that there are many technical aspects and concepts regarding SAP that take time to fully absorb, understand and become accustomed with. I am happy that I have stuck with it and I look forward to passing my certification attempt and applying for consultant roles with SAP. Thanks all

 

Stuart

entry.title: Stock transfer for stock type using /scwm/adgi
entry.content:
Hello colleagues,
I`m facing a situation and I couldn`t find anything related to it. This is my first question ever assigned in here.
We are using EWM decentralized with ECC.
Current situation: 
We do from time to time manual goods issue posting to an ECC storage location using /scwm/adgi
Issue: 
Users are able to select and post stocks with stock type S2.

How can I limit posting goods issue using tcode /scwm/adgi to ECC storage locations? 
For stock type Q2 if we do goods issue posting we receive a queue entry.

Best regards,
Sorin

entry.title: Re: How to find an API on SAP S/4HANA OP (EN)
entry.content:
Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is a concept.
"Premises" is a location.

"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

And Token URL : (uaa.url) https://{}.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
And Client ID : uaa.clientid
And clientsecret : uaa.clientsecret

I have added to my user the Process 
I can trigger workflows from the Monitoring instance with my user

entry.title: Re: How to implement Onboarding 2.0 without Employee Cent...
entry.content: RCM

entry.title: How do I adjust Fiori login error messages texts?
entry.content:
Hey folks,

I am developing a custom Fiori Launchpad login screen and a part of the requirements is to have slightly different error descriptions instead of the default ones ("Enter your user", "Enter a password", etc.), as on the following screenshot:

I have adjusted the login procedure as per various known guides, i.e., modified login procedure in SICF, created my custom class that inherits from /UI2/CL_SRA_LOGIN, made some tweaks in html templates, css files, INIT_DEFAULT_PROPERTIES method of my custom class, etc. - no problems with that. But it turned out that message texts aren't accessible from /UI2/CL_SRA_LOGIN or its descendants. I made some research and found out that those texts are fetched in private method 'LOAD_TEXT' of class CL_ICF_SYSTEM_LOGIN, which is a parent class of /UI2/CL_SRA_LOGIN, and the texts are pulled from textpool of that class (namely, texts 041 and 042). Since this method is private, I can't redefine it and modify the texts... Unfortunately, debugging of login screens is also quite complicated, as runtime execution is happening on client 000 (because we're not even logged in to any "normal" clients yet) and I don't have proper authorizations to do that.

Do you guys maybe know (or had the same problem) if there's a way to modify such texts? Any suggestions are welcome, even "dirty" ones.

entry.title: BI Commentary Application - DB and schema?
entry.content:
Dear all,

I want to explore the Commentary Application for my Webi Reports.

I see per default it's selected the Audit DB as the DB to store comments.

1) Do you know under what schema are these comments stored?

2) If I decided to choose a different DB, like HANA DB...in the options it gives to setup the connection (server, port, user, pwd...) I don't see mention to schema...do you know what schema name will it create in my HANA DB?

Thanks, best regards

Victor Torreblanca

kind regards

entry.title: Re: select option for given range
entry.content: It's only during the execution of the program that you may add SELECT ... WHERE lifnr IN SO_LIFNR and lifnr between '0000001000' and '0000002000', not in the select-options itself.

entry.title: Re: sapui5 application deploy error
entry.content:
Hi @Ryan-Crosby , the error is as follows.
"There is no Virus Scan profile indicated as “Default profile”."

entry.title: Visualize my code change from ADT local history (Eclipse, maybe not even ABAP specific)
entry.content:
Hey, is there a way to visualize my code changes from Eclipse/ADT local history?

I would like a Plugin or something that shows me how my code evolved overt time (adding features, refactoring...).
it also could give some statistics (lines of code, / per method, # of methods...)

Thanks
Joachim

#ABAP
#FirstPost
#Eclipse

entry.title: How we can top up gift cards in Sap Customer Checkout
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Hope you guys are doing well ....!

Here is an small flavor to ask could any one let us know what are the steps we should take to top up gift cards in sap customer checkout when top up enabled . 

Your prompt response will be really appreciated .

Thanks in advance .

Best Regards,

Tharindu.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content: To get screen fields updated make sure fields on the new custom screen are named with GS_KNVV-zfield

entry.title: Re: how to use discover api - cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use use based on you requirement.

once you copied your API from discovery you can apply the polices and target system configurations.

entry.content: Did my part!

entry.title: Re: Consuming external API from Ui5 application in S4 on...
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer ,

Thanks. I checked with the team responsible for the API and it didn't support OData protocol.

In this case I could use Http client to consume the external API. isn't it? do you see any issues in this approach? 

Regards,

Ratheesh BS

entry.title: Re: Sending mail to user when release code is updated in...
entry.content:
Hello @DharaniSriram05 

The requirement is strange to me as there is a standard functionality to implement it. But if you insist on reinventing the wheel you can use MM_PUR_S4_PO_POST or ME_PURCHDOC_POSTED BAdI enhancement to sent emails from purchase orders.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: sapui5 application deploy error
entry.content:
nice, thank you for your recommendation. 
regards.

entry.title: Re: Production order confirmation via custom design
entry.content:
Hello @alex_alex85 

I think we are mixing two different topics here:

  1. Decoupling Processes Carried out During Confirmation and 
  2. Reprocessing Confirmations

Decoupling Processes Carried out During Confirmation Decoupled processes are posted with CO1P. Decoupling is implemented to improve performance - a production confirmation can be saved quickly and the resulting processes e.g. cost determination can be posted later on. Such a confirmation is saved perfectly correctly even if it creates coupled operation for later posting. In that case BAPI shouldn't report any errors. Naturally, users need to be trained about decoupling, so they are not surprised that they see material movements or actual costs right after they save a confirmation.

Decoupling is customizable and I don't think you can tell if a confirmation will yield decoupled operations with the BAPI test run or before the BAPI is called and the confirmation is actually saved.

Reprocessing Confirmations That is the error pool I referred to in my previous posting. Errors are reprocessed with CO16N. Faulty confirmation are saved to the pool if the BAPI is called with POST_WRONG_ENTRIES = 1 or 2. If the BAPI is called with wrong data, that will result in an error, that can be checked with a test run. Notice, that the test run won't lock orders, so potential locking problems can't be verify this way.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to activate scope item 4X9 in S/4 HANA 2023 on-pr...
entry.content:
Hi Melai,

 

Did you find the solution? I am also looking forward to the solution.

 

Regards

Abid Ali

entry.title: Re: F2388 Monitor payments: Batch exist but payments not...
entry.content:
Try Maintaining following Authorizations in your User ID

 

ObjectFieldValue
F_REGU_BUKFBTCH23,26
F_REGU_KOAKOART* (All)
F_PAYRQORIGIN* (All)
F_PAYRQACTVT03
F_BL_BANKBUKRSCompany Code
F_BL_BANKHBKID* (All)
F_BL_BANKHKTID* (All)
F_BL_BANKZLSCH* (All)
F_BL_BANKACTVT03
P_ABAPCOARS2
P_ABAPREPIDRFMPAY00
F_LFA1_APPACTVT03
F_LFA1_APPAPPKZF
F_LFA1_BUKACTVT03
F_LFA1_BUKBUKRSCompany Code
F_BNK_RSNDACTVT01
S_BTCH_ADMBTCADMINY, D

entry.title: Re: Installation issue with SAP B1 SQL Version - Setup ha...
entry.content: I was facing the same issue with B11000_2305. The solution is to run install application from ComponentsWizard in Packages.x64 of the installation folder.

entry.title: Re: how to use discover api - cloud integration
entry.content:
Do I have to use the policy of package: Connect to SAP Business Technology Platform Services

 

after configuration, how do I call the api from my iflow?

entry.title: Re: select option for given range
entry.content: Instead using the standard data element you can use an own data element with an own domain containing only these values.

entry.title: Re: A Culinary Journey Through New York City
entry.content: Hey Seba, don't forget to do that 45 to 60-minute run to burn off all those calories afterward, okay? 😀

entry.title: Initial Balance Upload in One Exposure from Operations.
entry.content:
How to see the initial balances for the bank account in One Exposure from Operation?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Initial Balance Upload in One Exposure from Operations.
entry.content:
Define the memo record types in the SSCUI "Define Memo Record Types  (ID 101029 )".

Use the App Manage Memo records 2.0 to create the Actual cash memo records to create the initial balances for the Bank Accounts. Use the memo record type "Actual Cash Memo."

Help Portal Links: -

Manage Memo Records 2.0

Import Memo Records 2.0

 

Regards,

SAP Support
